title: Vela Registry Checkpoint (v0.1)
description: A registry operator's signed claim that at sequence N the registry held exactly the given set of entries, summarized by a content-addressed root over the canonical entry list. Consumers verify the signature against the operator's pubkey, recompute the root from the registry they hold, and assert the two agree. Forms a chain via `previous_checkpoint`; v0.148 federation cross-checks the chain across hubs.

registry_root.description: sha256 over canonical bytes of the alphabetically-sorted list of (vfr_id, latest_snapshot_hash, latest_event_log_hash, owner_pubkey, signature) tuples. Deterministic over the same registry state.

previous_checkpoint.description: Optional vrc_* id of the predecessor checkpoint. Omitted for sequence=0.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over the canonical preimage (checkpoint body with signature + checkpoint_id zeroed).
